top_categories.workout_context: Paris-Roubaix Challenge's cobblestoned sectors (pave) and paved roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 106 miles.
At mile 60 of 106, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Paris-Roubaix Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Paris-Roubaix Challenge, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
When someone attacks on cobblestoned sectors (pave) and paved roads, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on cobblestoned sectors (pave) and paved roads. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 106 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Paris-Roubaix Challenge's cobblestoned sectors (pave) and paved roads rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
106 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Smooth pedaling at 106 miles saves muscle. Bad technique at mile 10 is invisible. At mile 80 it's a cramp.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Paris-Roubaix Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,600ft of climbing on cobblestoned sectors (pave) and paved roads does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: April in Busigny to Roubaix Velodrome, Nord, France can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Paris-Roubaix Challenge. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Paris-Roubaix Challenge’s 106 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Paris-Roubaix Challenge demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride cobblestoned sectors (pave) and paved roads at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 106 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 106 miles of cobblestoned sectors (pave) and paved roads in Busigny to Roubaix Velodrome, Nord, France.
